title: OOF pit-window priors
summary: High-cardinality categorical encoding benchmarks and sklearn docs support cross-fitted target/statistical encodings to avoid leakage.
rationale: High-cardinality categorical encoding benchmarks and sklearn docs support cross-fitted target/statistical encodings to avoid leakage. Here, Driver, Race, Compound, Stint, TyreLife bins, and their combinations likely encode team/track pit windows beyond generic tyre age.
implementation_hint: Create smoothed out-of-fold encodings for categorical keys such as Race_Compound_TyreLifeBin, Driver_Stint_TyreLifeBin, Race_LapBin, Year_Race_Compound. Use K-fold or group-aware OOF means/counts on train; map test using full-train smoothed stats with global prior fallback.
expected_effect: May add strong race-specific hazard priors while keeping LightGBM/XGBoost compatible and avoiding naive target leakage.
risk: Random OOF folds can still overfit repeated race rows; grouped folds by Race or Year are safer but may lower local CV while improving leaderboard robustness.
